title: Establish an Operational Incident-Handling Capability
description: establish an operational incident-handling capability for organizational systems that includes preparation, detection, analysis, containment, recovery, and user response activities

checks.name: An Operational Incident-Handling Capability is Established
checks.purpose: Establishment of an operational incident-handling capability is an organizational GRC documentation concern documented in the incident response plan and is not technically verifiable via automated check

checks.name: The Incident-Handling Capability Includes Preparation
checks.purpose: Preparation activities such as training, tool readiness, and contact lists are organizational GRC documentation concerns and are not technically verifiable via automated check

checks.name: The Incident-Handling Capability Includes Detection
checks.purpose: Detection capabilities such as monitoring tools and alert thresholds are organizational GRC documentation concerns and are not technically verifiable via automated check

checks.name: The Incident-Handling Capability Includes Analysis
checks.purpose: Analysis procedures such as triage criteria and forensic processes are organizational GRC documentation concerns and are not technically verifiable via automated check

checks.name: The Incident-Handling Capability Includes Containment
checks.purpose: Containment procedures such as isolation steps and escalation paths are organizational GRC documentation concerns and are not technically verifiable via automated check

checks.name: The Incident-Handling Capability Includes Recovery
checks.purpose: Recovery procedures such as restoration steps and post-incident review processes are organizational GRC documentation concerns and are not technically verifiable via automated check

checks.name: The Incident-Handling Capability Includes User Response Activities
checks.purpose: User response activities such as user notification procedures and awareness training are organizational GRC documentation concerns and are not technically verifiable via automated check

remediation: Establish and document a formal incident response plan covering preparation, detection, analysis, containment, recovery, and user response activities. Ensure the plan is reviewed and approved by organizational leadership.
